results.title: Western Australia: the search for oil
results.creator_credit: Norman A. Uren and Western Australian Government Film Unit

video_links.name: Western Australia: the search for oil

results.brief_description: Shows the first operational stage of the search for oil at Exmouth Gulf (WA) including the seismograph party making exploratory drillings and echo soundings with explosives. (Explains the principle of this method with diagrams.) A highlight is the building of the large derrick before drilling for oil began.

results.title: Men without faces
results.creator_credit: Dennis A. Hill and New South Wales. Dept. of Prisons
results.brief_description: This film describes the NSW penal system and explains the efforts made by prison officers to rehabilitate prisoners. Shows how hardened prisoners enter Grafton Gaol, a prison of bars and locks, while those who show promise of reforming are sent to training centres in Goulbourn, Berrima and Emu Plains where they are taught trades and how to live in a community, preparation for the day of their release.

results.title: Tit for tat [Censored version]
results.creator_credit: Martha Ansara, Graeme Isaac, Film Australia and Mayfan
results.brief_description: Examines the world of tattoos and the surrounding culture of bikers and rebels as the filmmaker travels with Baldy, a biker and photo-journalist, to the Sandhurst Rally for the yearly Tattoo Exhibition and judging. There they meet AJ, the most tattooed man in Australia and a renowned tattoo artist himself. WARNING - SOME SCENES IN THIS PROGRAMME MAY OFFEND SOME VIEWERS.

results.title: Introducing badminton
results.creator_credit: Gillian Gilks, Mike Goodwin and Sports Films
results.brief_description: A coaching film which shows how to execute all the basic strokes of the game, badminton, and explains the basic tactics for singles, men’s doubles and mixed doubles. Intended for sports centres, schools and colleges.

results.title: Design for climate
results.creator_credit: Nicholas Alexander, S.T. Evans and Building Research and Development Advisory Committee
results.brief_description: Research on problems of building design for Australian conditions. Maintaining an acceptable indoor climate by economic means involves a building of suitable thermal characteristics using insulation and heating and cooling as required, proper lighting and ventilation. The noise problem, protection of external surfaces and relating the building to its environment for best use of the natural climate are considered.

results.title: Australia's New Faces 1969 Grand Final
results.creator_credit: Desmond Tester, John Proper, Peter Faiman and GTV9 Melbourne
results.brief_description: Out of 328 acts judged throughout 1969 are the 12 finalists. The winner will receive a $5,000 contract with the Nine Network. The winners - The Paul MacKay Sound; the judges - John Laws, Geoff Brook, Tom Miller, Pat London, Jim McKay. Hosted by Terry Dear and Frank Wilson, voice over by Pete Smith and sponsored by Kevin Dennis. Performing Contestants include Paul Mackay Sound and Mike McClellan.

results.title: Beaches and boats
results.creator_credit: Leith Goodall, Norman A. Uren and Western Australian Government Film Unit
results.brief_description: This film depicts the beauty of Western Australia’s beaches and the lifestyle that can be enjoyed there. Sandy beaches stretch along the West Australian coast for hundreds of miles, providing an ideal place for a holiday of boating, water-skiing, surfboard riding, and sunbathing.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Leith Goodall, Norman A. Uren and Western Australian Government Film Unit

results.title: Touring Car Champions for DOS
results.creator_credit: Torus Games and Virtual Sports Interactive
results.material_description: CD-ROM videogame. CD-ROM, CD jewel case and manual in cardboard box
results.brief_description:
Touring Car Champions is based on the Australian Touring Car Championship and the Bathurst 1000. The game features only one track, set in Bathurst, New South Wales. Interestingly, this is depicted through full motion video of the actual track, instead of being recreated or rendered in within the game itself. The video slows down or speeds up based on the player’s speed and the opponent’s cars are 3D models superimposed onto this video.

The game features three modes: The first is Practice, which has no opponents. Quick Race has the player choosing the difficulty of the opponents and racing in a pre-set car. Lastly, The Round is a season-spanning tournament where the player has to work their way up from the amateur division to eventual Touring Car Champion. Along the way, the player can decide what racers, pit crew and car parts to use in each race.
